original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE antimicrobial and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, children, and young people with impetigo, including reassessment after treatment or recurrent disease.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Community, primary care, and hospital reassessment of impetigo.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting worsening, non-improvement, serious-illness features, previous antimicrobial exposure, microbiology already obtained, result review, and clinician-led referral context. Antibiotic names, doses, durations, and treatment choices are excluded.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.7–1.1.9 — advice and reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, failure to improve after treatment, alternative-diagnosis concern, serious illness or cellulitis concern, and prior antibiotic exposure.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.11–1.1.14 — microbiology and result review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording skin or nasal swab context, frequent recurrence, and microbiology results already reviewed; it does not generate a test or antimicrobial change.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.15–1.1.16 — referral and specialist-advice criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting serious illness, widespread disease with immunocompromise, bullous disease in infancy, recurrence, systemic illness, complication risk, and an already made referral or specialist discussion.

title: Impetigo: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE antimicrobial and referral adaptation
population: Adults, children, and young people with impetigo, including reassessment after treatment or recurrent disease.
intended_setting: Community, primary care, and hospital reassessment of impetigo.
